oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with feature-specific fields) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact strings '<' and '>' must be inserted around the URL value when the href contains spaces, resulting in a format like '(url with spaces)'.
The exact string '- - -' with single spaces between hyphens must be rendered to represent the horizontal rule when the horizontal_rule option is set to 'dashes'.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_block_structure, feature2_inline_formatting, feature3_links, feature4_images, feature5_code_blocks, feature6_lists, feature7_tables, feature8_output_style_options, feature9_element_omission. Feature descriptions include: Converts block-level HTML text structure into Markdown block syntax while normalizing surrounding whitespace.; Converts inline emphasis and inline code elements into Markdown inline syntax without adding artificial block breaks.; Converts anchor elements into Markdown links, including title attributes, whitespace trimming around link text, URL escaping, and reference-style output when requested.; Converts image elements into Markdown image syntax using source, alternate text, and title attributes, including URL escaping for spaces.; Converts preformatted code blocks into fenced Markdown code blocks and includes a language label when the HTML provides one.; Converts ordered and unordered lists into Markdown list rows, respecting configured marker and spacing where provided.; Converts table captions and table body rows into a plain Markdown table-like row stream while empty tables produce no output.; Applies formatting options that change Markdown style while preserving the converted content semantics.; Allows callers to request that selected element names be omitted from the rendered Markdown output.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: html=[' Link 1 Example ', '

Heading 2

Heading 3 println!("Hello"); ', '

    '], options=['{dict}']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the setext style definition for headings
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to using '===' and '---' separators below a line of underscores and dashes to render headings, rather than the block-level text separators used elsewhere.

feature1_block_structure.json.description: Converts block-level HTML text structure into Markdown block syntax while normalizing surrounding whitespace.

cases.expected_output:

## Heading 2

### Heading 3

feature2_inline_formatting.json.description: Converts inline emphasis and inline code elements into Markdown inline syntax without adding artificial block breaks.

feature3_links.json.description: Converts anchor elements into Markdown links, including title attributes, whitespace trimming around link text, URL escaping, and reference-style output when requested.

feature4_images.json.description: Converts image elements into Markdown image syntax using source, alternate text, and title attributes, including URL escaping for spaces.

feature5_code_blocks.json.description: Converts preformatted code blocks into fenced Markdown code blocks and includes a language label when the HTML provides one.

feature6_lists.json.description: Converts ordered and unordered lists into Markdown list rows, respecting configured marker and spacing where provided.

feature7_tables.json.description: Converts table captions and table body rows into a plain Markdown table-like row stream while empty tables produce no output.

feature8_output_style_options.json.description: Applies formatting options that change Markdown style while preserving the converted content semantics.

feature9_element_omission.json.description: Allows callers to request that selected element names be omitted from the rendered Markdown output.

_constraints.output_rule: Convert <h1> to #, <h2> to ##, ..., <h6> to ###### with a blank line separator between each heading
Convert <i> and <em> contents to _text_ (underscores only)
No nesting handling mentioned; imply treated as separate units or outermost takes precedence based on sequential output
Wrap content in `backticks`

_constraints.input_condition: Inline code content containing a backtick character
_constraints.output_rule: The test case does not specify behavior, but implies standard escaping or data fidelity if content is preserved in a literal sense, however strict instruction says expand, implying backticks are preserved as is if no full range, this constraint covers default behavior for simple code
Convert to [text](url) format

_constraints.input_condition: HTML <a> tag with href containing spaces
_constraints.output_rule: Enclose URL in angle brackets: (url)

_constraints.input_condition: HTML <a> tag with visible whitespace around text
_constraints.output_rule: Trim whitespace from link text boundaries
Include title in quotes immediately after URL: (url "title")
No space between them unless inherent to link text trimming
Convert to ![alt](src)
Use alt text as image label
Include title in parentheses after source: (src "title")
Emit sequentially without extra status text or gaps between adjacent images
Enclose source in angle brackets
Wrap in fence marks
Include language before fence: XX
Format as bullet points with asterisk and configurable spacing
Format as numbered list ascending
Use specified number of spaces after marker
Output empty or skip header rows? Test cases show HEADERS NOT INCLUDED, only body rows
Format as pipe-delimited rows: | col1 | col2 |
Preserve spaces as-is in cells (no wrapping)
Empty output string
Render heading as text followed by separator line (====)
Render hr as - - - with spacing around

Render
as \\ (backslash)

Separate
segments with backslash newlines

_constraints.input_condition: Options object has omit_elements array containing a tag name
_constraints.output_rule: Omit the rendered markdown for that specific element tag

_constraints.input_condition: Input is JSON read from stdin
_constraints.output_rule: Must match the 'input' key in the JSON object
Decode options and pass to core logic
Print result to stdout
Read from `public_test_cases`
Run each case in order
Write to `rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/featureX@000.txt`
Write to `rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/featureX@NNN.txt`
